reforms.description: Norway's tax-break programme, SkatteFUNN, serves as the primary support mechanism for business innovation, particularly benefiting early-stage innovative enterprises through refundable tax credits.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The programme enhances R&D activity among innovative firms, thereby fostering productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme is described as the backbone of Norway's support for business innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The SkatteFUNN programme is a significant structural reform that underpins the innovation ecosystem by providing essential financial support to businesses engaged in R&D.
reforms.source_quote: A tax-break programme (SkatteFUNN) forms the backbone of Norway’s support for business innovation.

reforms.description: The government has proposed increasing support for innovative firms through the core programme and continued use of a competitive-bid approach for research grants, promoting SkatteFUNN.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for innovative firms is expected to enhance R&D activities, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has proposed increasing support for innovative firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant enhancement of existing funding mechanisms aimed at fostering innovation among firms, which is crucial for economic competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The 2015 Budget proposed increasing support via the core programme, continued use of a competitive-bid approach for research grants, and promoting SkatteFUNN.

reforms.description: The government has launched an evaluation of policies promoting university-business linkages, including an assessment of whether technology transfer offices require more powers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing university-business collaboration can lead to increased innovation and commercialization of research outputs.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has launched an evaluation of policies promoting university-business linkages.
reforms.importance_rationale: This evaluation is significant as it aims to strengthen the mechanisms for technology transfer, which is crucial for enhancing innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The government has launched an evaluation of policy promoting university-business linkages, including assessment of whether technology transfer offices require more powers.

reforms.description: Norway has implemented the SkatteFUNN tax credit, which allows SMEs to claim 20% of R&D-related costs and larger enterprises to claim 18%, with the possibility of a tax refund if the credit exceeds the firm's total tax liability.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credit incentivizes R&D investment by reducing the financial burden on firms, thereby promoting innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit is actively utilized by SMEs and larger enterprises in Norway.
reforms.importance_rationale: The SkatteFUNN tax credit represents a significant structural change in how R&D is funded in Norway, encouraging a broader base of firms to engage in innovative activities.
reforms.source_quote: SkatteFUNN is a tax credit; 20% of R&D-related costs for SMEs and 18% for larger enterprises.

reforms.description: The Research Council of Norway runs large-scale grant programmes that provide competitive thematic research grants to support various research initiatives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These grants facilitate applied research that can lead to innovations and improvements in various sectors, thus contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Research Council actively administers these grant programmes.
reforms.importance_rationale: The grant programmes are significant in scale and scope, providing essential funding for thematic research that aligns with national priorities.
reforms.source_quote: Grant programmes run by the Research Council of Norway include competitive thematic research grant programmes.

reforms.description: Norway is reviewing its full menu of innovation support measures to improve efficiency and address overlaps between schemes, which could enhance the overall effectiveness of innovation policy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving the efficiency of innovation support measures can lead to better allocation of resources, fostering innovation and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: A review of innovation support measures is due to take place.
reforms.importance_rationale: The review aims to enhance the effectiveness of existing innovation policies, which is significant for improving Norway's innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: It is a timely moment for a review of the full menu of innovation support measures.

reforms.description: Reforms to funding are being implemented to reshape research incentives in both the higher education and research institute sectors, aiming to enhance research capacities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reshaping research incentives can lead to more effective use of public funds, enhancing research output and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Reforms to funding are being used to reshape research incentives.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms aim to enhance the effectiveness of research funding, which is crucial for improving Norway's research landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Reforms to funding are being used to reshape research incentives in both the higher education and research institutes sectors.

reforms.description: Norway is implementing targeted support for innovation to enhance domestic innovation capabilities and strengthen the country's absorptive capacity for global knowledge.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging domestic innovation is expected to lead to productivity growth and better integration of global technological advancements.
reforms.status_evidence: The text indicates a focus on promoting domestic innovation as a key policy area.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance Norway's innovation landscape, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Targeted support for innovation... encouraging domestic innovation is important and strengthens Norway’s absorptive capacity for global knowledge.

reforms.description: The SkatteFUNN tax break is Norway's largest R&D support programme, providing a tax credit for eligible R&D expenditures, particularly benefiting small and medium enterprises. The scheme has been expanded to increase support for larger-scale innovation and improve access to funding for innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax break incentivizes R&D investment, particularly among SMEs, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The SkatteFUNN tax break has been operational and is the primary innovation-related tax break in Norway.
reforms.importance_rationale: As the largest R&D support programme, SkatteFUNN plays a crucial role in enhancing the innovation capacity of Norwegian firms, particularly SMEs, and is integral to the national innovation strategy.
reforms.source_quote: The SkatteFUNN tax break is Norway’s largest R&D support programme and its only innovation-related tax break.

reforms.description: Recent reforms to block-grant funding aim to improve research incentives by linking funding allocations to research activity and implementing performance agreements. These changes are designed to enhance the effectiveness of public research funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning funding with research performance, the reforms are expected to enhance the quality and impact of public research, contributing to overall productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms to block-grant funding have been initiated to reshape research incentives.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant adjustment to the funding mechanism, aiming to improve research outcomes and align public funding with strategic priorities.
reforms.source_quote: Recent reforms to block-grant funding aim to improve research incentives with new components added to the block allocation linked to research activity.

reforms.description: The Norwegian government has initiated a funding reform in the research-institute sector, aiming to simplify budget allocation and enhance scientific quality and collaboration among research institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving funding mechanisms, the reform is expected to enhance research quality and collaboration, ultimately fostering innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding reform has been put into effect to induce change in the research-institute sector.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in how research institutes are funded, which is crucial for enhancing Norway's research landscape and innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: Funding of research institutes is complicated because several ministries are involved. A common performance-based mechanism for one segment of budget allocation aiming, inter alia, to strengthen scientific quality and collaboration was introduced.

reforms.description: Norway aims to enhance the impact of its R&D tax credit, which plays a central role in supporting innovation across various sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The R&D tax credit incentivizes private sector investment in innovation, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has indicated plans to work further on ensuring impact from the R&D tax credit.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it targets a key instrument for innovation support, potentially enhancing the effectiveness of existing policies.
reforms.source_quote: Work further on ensuring impact from the R&D tax credit given its central role in supporting innovation.

reforms.description: Norway plans to reduce the number of targeted innovation support programmes and review their focus to improve efficiency and effectiveness.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining innovation support can enhance resource allocation and improve the overall impact of public funding on innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has indicated plans to reduce the number of targeted innovation support programmes and review their focus.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses the efficiency of public innovation support, which is crucial for maximizing the impact of government investments.
reforms.source_quote: Reduce the number of targeted innovation support programmes and review their focus as per the OECD Innovation Review.
